title: Generated Build Contract
description: Authoritative shape for an AMOF-generated build contract. Generated builds are produced by AMOF detection + templating for codebases that do not ship a trustworthy upstream build instruction. See contracts/generated-build-contract.md.

build_contract_kind.description: Discriminator: this artifact came from the generated lane, not the existing lane.

ecosystem.description: Optional ecosystem id this artifact is scoped to. Omit for cross-ecosystem records.

source_repo.description: Where the source under analysis lives.

service.description: Optional service name within the repo. Required for multi-service repos; omit for single-service repos.

runtime_family.description: Detected runtime family. See generated-build-runtime-detector-matrix.md for the active taxonomy.

confidence.description: How strong the detection evidence is. `accepted` is the gating threshold for status >= proposed.

signals.description: Detection evidence. Hard signals are deterministic file presence checks. Soft signals are heuristics (script names, dependency hints).

hard.description: Files/markers whose presence proves the runtime family. E.g. `pyproject.toml`, `package.json`, `go.mod`.

value.description: Optional captured detail (e.g. python_version=3.12, node_version=20, go_module_name=...).

soft.description: Heuristic signals such as `start` script names, common entrypoint filenames, dependency hints. Used to break ties only.

id.description: Template id, e.g. python-uvicorn-distroless-v1, node-express-distroless-v1, go-stdlib-distroless-v1.

rendered_path.description: Where the rendered Dockerfile was written. Optional in proposed status until the template is materialized.

image_outputs.description: The image references the build would produce. At least one entry required.

inferred_port.description: Port the entrypoint is expected to expose. Required if the runtime family is a network service.

entrypoint.description: Final command emitted into the Dockerfile. Required for network services.

risk_flags.description: Caveats the operator must see. Examples: `multi_language_signals`, `custom_toolchain_heuristic`, `vendored_dependencies`, `large_context_size`.

refusal_reason.description: Required when status == refused. Explains which detection signal failed or which template was unsupported.
